title: Path Sum II
problem_statement: Find all root-to-leaf paths where the sum of node values equals targetSum.
constraints: The number of nodes is in the range [0, 5000]
intuition: Use DFS with a path list and backtracking when exploring root-to-leaf paths.

approaches.name: DFS with path recording
approaches.description: Traverse the tree recursively and track the current path sum, adding valid paths at leaf nodes.
approaches.time: O(n^2) in worst case due to path copies
approaches.why_not: Using BFS is possible but more cumbersome for path tracking.

interview_qa.q: How do you handle path state?
interview_qa.a: Use backtracking to add and remove nodes from the current path.

interview_qa.q: When do you add a path to results?
interview_qa.a: When you reach a leaf and the remaining sum equals the leaf value.
